entry.title: MAD-HTLC
entry.summary:
In the discussion involving transaction confirmations and blockchain reorganizations (reorgs), a few critical considerations emerge about ensuring transaction integrity. Specifically, there is an analysis of the implications of reorgs on the confirmation of transactions within a specified time frame. The concept revolves around needing additional blocks as a safety margin to prevent accusations of bribery, particularly concerning the parties involved in the transaction, referred to as Bob and Alice. This safety buffer would effectively extend the period required for confirming a transaction to mitigate the risks associated with potential reorgs.
The strategy appears feasible if the window for transaction confirmation is extended beyond a few hours. This duration accommodates the unlikelihood of experiencing reorgs that span more than three to four blocks. However, complications arise if the timeframe for transaction confirmation is inherently short, which could amplify the challenges in maintaining transaction validity amidst possible reorgs. This insight underlines the delicate balance required in managing blockchain integrity and transaction security, particularly in scenarios susceptible to reorgs.
